item.name: Append Blobs

description.content: Operations specific to append blobs including append block

item.name: Azure Blob Storage Append block

request.name: Azure Blob Storage Append block

description.content: Commits a new block of data to the end of an existing append blob. Each block can be up to 4 MiB in size. An append blob can contain up to 50,000 blocks.

variable.description: (Required) The container name. Must be a valid DNS name, 3 to 63 characters, starting with a letter or number, and containing only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
The name of the Azure Storage account

header.description: (Required) Specifies the version of the Azure Storage REST API to use for the request.

(Required) URL of the source data to append.

item.name: Blobs

description.content: Operations on blobs including get, put, delete, and metadata

item.name: Azure Blob Storage Put blob

request.name: Azure Blob Storage Put blob

description.content: Creates a new blob or replaces an existing blob within a container. The type of blob to create is specified in the x-ms-blob-type header. For block blobs up to 5000 MiB, the entire content can be uploaded in a single request.

variable.description: (Required) The container name. Must be a valid DNS name, 3 to 63 characters, starting with a letter or number, and containing only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
(Required) The blob name. Can contain any combination of characters and may include path separators (/) for organizing blobs in a virtual directory hierarchy.
The name of the Azure Storage account

header.description: (Required) Specifies the version of the Azure Storage REST API to use for the request.
(Required) The type of blob to create.
The length of the request body in bytes. Required for page blobs (set to 0 with x-ms-blob-content-length for the actual size).
The content type to set for the blob.
The tier to set on the blob.
Required for page blobs. Specifies the maximum size for the page blob, up to 8 TiB. Must be aligned to a 512-byte boundary.

variable.description: (Required) The container name. Must be a valid DNS name, 3 to 63 characters, starting with a letter or number, and containing only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
(Required) The blob name. Can contain any combination of characters and may include path separators (/) for organizing blobs in a virtual directory hierarchy.
The name of the Azure Storage account

The length of the request body in bytes. Required for page blobs (set to 0 with x-ms-blob-content-length for the actual size).
The MIME content type of the blob.
The content type to set for the blob.
The tier to set on the blob.
Required for page blobs. Specifies the maximum size for the page blob, up to 8 TiB. Must be aligned to a 512-byte boundary.

The name of the Azure Storage account

item.name: Azure Blob Storage Delete blob

request.name: Azure Blob Storage Delete blob

description.content: Marks the specified blob or snapshot for deletion. The blob is later deleted during garbage collection. If soft delete is enabled, the blob can be restored within the retention period.

query.description: The snapshot identifier. When specified, only the snapshot is deleted.

variable.description: (Required) The container name. Must be a valid DNS name, 3 to 63 characters, starting with a letter or number, and containing only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
(Required) The blob name. Can contain any combination of characters and may include path separators (/) for organizing blobs in a virtual directory hierarchy.
The name of the Azure Storage account

Required if the blob has associated snapshots. Specifies whether to delete the blob and all snapshots (include) or only the snapshots (only).

item.name: Azure Blob Storage Set blob tier

request.name: Azure Blob Storage Set blob tier

description.content: Sets the access tier of a block blob or page blob. For block blobs, supported tiers are Hot, Cool, Cold, and Archive. Moving a blob from Archive tier requires rehydration, which can take hours.

variable.description: (Required) The container name. Must be a valid DNS name, 3 to 63 characters, starting with a letter or number, and containing only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
The name of the Azure Storage account

header.description: (Required) Specifies the version of the Azure Storage REST API to use for the request.
(Required) The access tier to set for the blob.
Priority of rehydration when moving a blob out of the Archive tier. Standard priority may take up to 15 hours, High priority within 1 hour at a higher cost.

item.name: Azure Blob Storage Put block

request.name: Azure Blob Storage Put block

description.content: Uploads a block to be committed as part of a block blob. Each block can be up to 4000 MiB in size. The block is identified by a base64-encoded block ID. Blocks must be committed using Put Block List to form the final blob.

query.description: (Required) A base64-encoded block ID that identifies the block. All block IDs for a given blob must be the same length.

variable.description: (Required) The container name. Must be a valid DNS name, 3 to 63 characters, starting with a letter or number, and containing only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
The name of the Azure Storage account

item.name: Azure Blob Storage Lease container

request.name: Azure Blob Storage Lease container

description.content: Establishes and manages a lock on a container for delete operations. The lock duration can be infinite or between 15 and 60 seconds. Lease actions include acquire, renew, change, release, and break.

Proposed lease ID in GUID format.

item.name: Page Blobs

description.content: Operations specific to page blobs including put page and get page ranges

item.name: Azure Blob Storage Put page

request.name: Azure Blob Storage Put page

description.content: Writes a range of pages to a page blob. Pages must be aligned to 512-byte boundaries. The update action writes data to the blob, and the clear action deallocates the specified pages.

variable.description: (Required) The container name. Must be a valid DNS name, 3 to 63 characters, starting with a letter or number, and containing only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
The name of the Azure Storage account

header.description: (Required) Specifies the version of the Azure Storage REST API to use for the request.
(Required) The page write operation type.
(Required) The byte range for the page write operation. Must be aligned to 512-byte boundaries. Format is bytes=startByte-endByte.

variable.description: (Required) The container name. Must be a valid DNS name, 3 to 63 characters, starting with a letter or number, and containing only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
The name of the Azure Storage account

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

header.description: (Required) Specifies the version of the Azure Storage REST API to use for the request.
(Required) The page write operation type.
(Required) The byte range for the page write operation. Must be aligned to 512-byte boundaries. Format is bytes=startByte-endByte.

item.name: Azure Blob Storage Find blobs by tags

description.content: Finds blobs in the storage account whose tags match a given search expression. The search expression uses a SQL-like syntax against blob index tags.

description.content: The name of the Azure Storage account
(Required) The blob name. Can contain any combination of characters and may include path separators (/) for organizing blobs in a virtual directory hierarchy.
(Required) The container name. Must be a valid DNS name, 3 to 63 characters, starting with a letter or number, and containing only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.

The Azure Blob Storage REST API provides programmatic access to unstructured data storage in Azure. It supports operations on the Blob service, containers, and blobs, including block blobs, append blobs, and page blobs. Blobs can store any type of text or binary data such as documents, media files, and application installers. Blob Storage is optimized for storing massive amounts of unstructured data accessible via HTTP or HTTPS from anywhere in the world.
